title: Erucastrum palustre
intro: Erucastrum palustre, also known as marsh rocket, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its bright green leaves and small, white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Erucastrum palustre include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Erucastrum palustre include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Erucastrum palustre is classified within the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Erucastrum. The species is native to certain regions of the world. Erucastrum palustre is further classified within the order Brassicales. Its classification also includes being a member of the class Magnoliopsida.
